steps.title: Convert to a per-unit multiplier
steps.content:
Let $k=b^{1/n}$. Then $f(x)=ab^{x/n}$ becomes $f(x)=ak^x$, so $k$ is the multiplier for each 1-unit increase in $x$. Divide the two known function values:
$$\frac{f(7)}{f(4)}=\frac{135}{5}=27$$
The input increased by $3$, so $k^3=27$ and $k=3$.

steps.title: Use Desmos regression or multiply forward
steps.content:
In Desmos, enter the known points and regress $y_1\sim ak^{x_1}$; the model then evaluates directly at $x=9$. By hand, from $x=7$ to $x=9$ is two unit steps, so multiply by $3^2=9$:
$$f(9)=135\cdot 9=1215$$
The answer is 1215.
